name: 优化 ASR 性能与稳定性

description: Added ASR ready-latency benchmark script; run timed out due lingering websocket/error path even though median ready latency parsed at 520ms.

asi.hypothesis: Measure ASR session startup latency to validate stability improvements in live mode.
asi.rollback_reason: Benchmark process did not terminate cleanly (timeout), so result is unreliable for keep/discard.
asi.next_action_hint: Fix benchmark teardown by waiting for close or force-closing session; then rerun for stable metric.

name: Live模式 ASR/TTS 稳定性与响应优化

description: Baseline: measured median ASR session ready latency (3 runs) with current live pipeline.

asi.hypothesis: Current live ASR startup latency is the baseline before applying stability/retry optimizations.
asi.notes: No audio sent in this benchmark; measures session-ready responsiveness only.

description: Added ASR request timeout/retry and TTS watchdog recovery in LiveRoomWorkspace; benchmarked ASR ready latency worsened (median 482ms vs 413ms baseline).

asi.hypothesis: Retry/timeout handling and TTS watchdog should improve live stability without regressing ASR startup latency.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed relative to baseline on current benchmark workload.
asi.next_action_hint: Try backend relay/session-level optimizations (faster ready path) and benchmark with real speech payload + end-to-end turn latency.
asi.learning: Frontend resilience changes likely do not improve server-side ASR ready latency; need stability metric in addition to startup latency.

description: Reduced ASR relay init fallback from fixed 250ms to configurable default 120ms and centralized ready-path logic; improved median session-ready latency.

asi.hypothesis: Lowering init fallback wait should reduce live ASR startup latency while preserving ack-based readiness.
asi.changes: Added markSessionReady helper and VOLCENGINE_ASR_INIT_FALLBACK_MS default=120.
asi.caveat: Benchmark measures session-ready latency (no speech payload), not full transcription quality.

description: Improved relay finish behavior: close session directly when no audio was sent to avoid backend 1012 errors and reduce no-speech instability.

asi.hypothesis: Avoiding empty final frame for no-audio sessions improves stability and slightly reduces ready-to-close latency.
asi.changes: Added sentAudio tracking and no-audio close path in finishRealtimeAsrSession.
asi.stability_gain: Removed recurring 'No valid data found in input audio' error path in no-speech turns.

description: Added guarded adaptive init fallback (default 90ms + delayed guard when pending audio exists) to improve ASR ready responsiveness while reducing early-flush risk under real speech.

asi.hypothesis: Lower fallback should reduce startup latency; guard delay when pending audio protects stability from premature ready.
asi.changes: VOLCENGINE_ASR_INIT_FALLBACK_MS default 90, VOLCENGINE_ASR_INIT_GUARDED_DELAY_MS default 80, two-stage timer in open handler.
asi.stability_expectation: If user audio arrives before ACK, ready emission can be delayed by guarded window to avoid too-early flush.

description: Cached ASR env/config values in relay hot path to reduce per-session setup overhead and avoid repeated env parsing.

asi.hypothesis: Eliminating repeated requiredEnv/optionalEnv lookups in session creation and init payload can reduce startup overhead.
asi.changes: Added cached getAsrEnvConfig() and reused appId/accessToken/cluster/resourceId across createRealtimeAsrSession/buildInitPayload.
asi.risk_check: Configuration still read from env once per process; no protocol behavior changes.

description: New baseline for anti-overfitting workload: ASR startup latency when audio chunk is sent immediately (pending-audio path).

asi.hypothesis: Pending-audio startup benchmark better reflects real live usage and guards against overfitting to no-audio ready latency.
asi.workload: start session -> immediate chunk send -> wait ready

description: Tried reducing pending-audio guarded delay default 80->40ms to speed ready under immediate chunk send; no improvement on pending-audio benchmark.

asi.hypothesis: Smaller guarded delay should lower pending-audio startup latency.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed (331ms vs 328ms baseline) and variance suggests no reliable gain.
asi.next_action_hint: Test bypassing guarded delay when ACK already observed/open warm path, or instrument ack-vs-fallback split before tuning constants.
asi.observation: Latency spread widened; aggressive guard reduction may increase timing jitter.

description: Added single-chunk guarded delay path (default 25ms) for pending-audio startup: keeps conservative delay for multi-chunk backpressure but speeds common first-chunk live case.

asi.hypothesis: Most live starts have one pending chunk; a shorter guard for this case should improve startup while retaining stability for heavier pending queues.
asi.changes: New env VOLCENGINE_ASR_INIT_SINGLE_CHUNK_GUARD_MS (default 25) and dynamic pendingGuard selection.
asi.stability_note: Multi-chunk pending sessions still use full guarded delay, limiting premature flush risk.

description: Tried rolling ACK-latency adaptive fallback (p50-based) for init ready timer; worsened pending-audio startup latency.

asi.hypothesis: Use recent ACK latencies to auto-tune init fallback and reduce startup delay without sacrificing stability.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary pending-audio metric regressed (347ms vs 328ms baseline).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep static guarded defaults for now; pursue end-to-end live-turn benchmark and SSE/TTS recovery metrics next.
asi.learning: Adaptive fallback introduced extra variance and slower median in this workload.

description: Cached static ASR init payload template (app/audio/request base) and only injected per-session uid/reqid to reduce session start object construction overhead.

asi.hypothesis: Reducing per-session init payload allocations and env-derived field assembly lowers pending-audio startup latency.
asi.changes: Added getStaticInitPayload cache and lightweight buildInitPayload(session).
asi.tradeoff: No protocol behavior change; relies on process-level static env config.

description: Tuned relay init fallback default 90ms -> 80ms (guarded path unchanged), yielding faster pending-audio startup latency.

asi.hypothesis: After adding single-chunk guard, reducing base fallback to 80ms should cut startup delay without destabilizing pending-flush timing.
asi.changes: VOLCENGINE_ASR_INIT_FALLBACK_MS default changed to 80ms; guard defaults unchanged.
asi.note: Still benchmarking pending-audio start path to avoid no-audio overfitting.

description: Further tuned relay init fallback default 80ms -> 70ms for faster pending-audio startup; median improved but with one high-latency outlier.

asi.hypothesis: Lower base fallback to 70ms should reduce ready latency in common live startup path.
asi.outlier_note: Observed one large outlier (931ms); median still improved vs previous 181ms and baseline 328ms.
asi.next_action_hint: Re-run same config once to validate variance and ensure gain is stable.

description: Stability re-run of 70ms fallback config regressed heavily; indicates higher variance/instability despite prior fast run.

asi.hypothesis: The 70ms fallback tuning would remain consistently faster across repeated runs.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worse than baseline in confirmation run (372ms vs 328ms baseline), suggesting unstable benefit.
asi.next_action_hint: Revert to safer 80ms fallback (or test 75ms) and prioritize end-to-end live-turn benchmark before further micro-tuning.
asi.learning: 70ms appears too aggressive under current network/runtime jitter.

description: Reverted fallback default from 70ms back to 80ms after instability check; pending-audio startup recovered to stable improved range.

asi.hypothesis: 80ms fallback provides better stability/latency tradeoff than aggressive 70ms.
asi.changes: Default INIT_FALLBACK_MS set back to 80ms; guarded delays unchanged.
asi.stability_result: No extreme outlier observed; substantially better than baseline 328ms.

description: Tuned single-chunk guarded delay default 25ms -> 20ms while keeping fallback at 80ms; improved pending-audio startup latency.

asi.hypothesis: Slightly shorter single-chunk guard reduces startup wait in common live first-chunk path without destabilizing multi-chunk behavior.
asi.stability_note: Multi-chunk sessions still use full guarded delay (80ms).

description: Tried single-chunk guard 15ms (from 20ms); significantly worse than current best configuration on pending-audio startup.

asi.hypothesis: Further reducing single-chunk guard might yield extra startup gains.
asi.rollback_reason: Metric regressed sharply versus current best (315ms vs 180ms), indicating over-aggressive guard harms stability/latency consistency.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep 20ms as current sweet spot; move to end-to-end live-turn benchmark and TTS-side reliability metrics.
asi.learning: Too-small single-chunk guard likely increases timing races/jitter.

description: Baseline for anti-overfitting multi-pending workload: startup latency with 3 audio chunks queued before ready.

asi.hypothesis: Need a second workload baseline (multi-pending) to ensure tuning generalizes beyond single-chunk startup path.
asi.workload: start session -> queue 3 chunks -> wait ready

description: Tried lowering multi-pending guarded delay default 80ms -> 70ms; did not improve multi-pending startup latency.

asi.hypothesis: Reducing guarded delay for multi-chunk pending could speed startup in buffered scenarios.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric regressed vs multi-pending baseline (316ms vs 302ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep 80ms guarded delay for multi-pending path; explore reducing fallback or improving flush scheduling instead.
asi.learning: Multi-pending path appears to need more conservative guard for stable startup.

description: Tried lowering base init fallback 80ms -> 70ms for multi-pending workload; caused clear regression.

asi.hypothesis: Reducing base fallback would reduce total wait before ready in multi-pending path.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened significantly vs baseline (350ms vs 302ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep fallback at 80ms for multi-pending stability and avoid further aggressive timing cuts; shift focus to end-to-end turn benchmark and resilience metrics.
asi.learning: Multi-pending startup is more sensitive to too-early fallback timing.

description: Tried changing guard semantics from additive delay to elapsed-time budget (remaining guard) to reduce multi-pending wait; regressed multi-pending startup latency.

asi.hypothesis: Applying guard as a minimum-from-open budget (instead of adding a second full delay) would lower ready latency for multi-pending sessions.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary metric worsened versus multi-pending baseline (335ms vs 302ms).
asi.next_action_hint: Keep current additive guard model; focus next on SSE reconnect resilience and end-to-end turn benchmark instrumentation.
asi.learning: The elapsed-budget approach likely triggers less favorable backend timing despite lower theoretical wait.

description: Increased multi-pending guarded delay default 80ms -> 90ms to favor startup stability under queued-chunk conditions; improved median on multi-pending workload.

asi.hypothesis: A slightly more conservative guard for pending>1 chunks can reduce backend timing races and improve stable startup latency.
asi.anti_overfit: Applied on multi-pending benchmark (3 queued chunks), not single-chunk path.

description: Refined multi-pending timing profile: reduced base fallback 80ms -> 75ms while keeping guarded delay 90ms; improved startup latency on queued-chunk workload.

asi.hypothesis: With guarded delay increased to 90ms, fallback can be slightly reduced to 75ms for better responsiveness without reintroducing race-related regressions.
asi.anti_overfit: Validated on multi-pending benchmark (3 queued chunks).

description: Confirmation rerun of 75/90/20 timing profile regressed above baseline; prior improvement appears unstable under noise.

asi.hypothesis: The 75ms fallback + 90ms guard config would hold up on repeat multi-pending runs.
asi.rollback_reason: Repeat run was worse than baseline (325ms vs 302ms), indicating the previous gain was not stable enough.
asi.next_action_hint: Rollback to 80/90/20 profile and prioritize end-to-end live-turn benchmark + resilience metrics over further micro-timing tweaks.
asi.learning: Multi-pending timing remains jitter-sensitive; single-run gains can be misleading.

description: Baseline for mixed anti-overfitting workload: combined single-pending and multi-pending startup latency (mean of medians).

asi.hypothesis: A mixed-workload metric better reflects real live variance and reduces overfitting risk versus single-path tuning.
